persona.name: Tyler Kowalski

occupation.title: Electrical Apprentice
occupation.organization: Kowalski & Sons Electrical, a family-run contracting business in Scranton, PA
occupation.description: Learning the trade from his uncle. Runs conduit, pulls wire, and handles basic residential work under supervision. Physical, dirty work that has him sweating and covered in drywall dust most days. Started right after high school graduation.

persona.education: High school diploma from Dunmore High School, 2025. Took shop classes and auto tech. Decent grades but never considered college seriously — saw his uncle making good money in the trades and decided that was the smarter path.
long_term_goals: Get his journeyman electrician license by age 22
Buy a truck — specifically a used F-150
Eventually take over the family business from his uncle
Build a house or at least a nice garage for his tools and truck
persona.style: Tyler communicates in short, blunt sentences with a Northeast PA accent. He doesn't sugarcoat things and finds long explanations unnecessary. He wears work boots, Carhartt pants, and whatever t-shirt was on top of the pile. His hands are permanently a little rough and stained. He's not trying to impress anyone and is genuinely confused by people who spend money on their face. Friendly enough but won't go out of his way to make conversation about things he doesn't care about.

traits: Straightforward and unfiltered — says what he thinks
Hands-on learner who respects practical skills
Dismissive of things he sees as frivolous or vain
Loyal to family and close friends
Stubborn about his habits and opinions
Physically tough and proud of it

interests: Hunting and fishing in the Poconos
Call of Duty and Madden with friends online
Country music — Morgan Wallen, Luke Combs
likes: Cold beer after a long day on the job site
Things that work without being complicated
His uncle's no-BS approach to life
Friday nights at the local bar with coworkers
Cheap, effective products — Irish Spring, Old Spice
dislikes: Being told what to buy by ads or influencers
The idea of spending $30 on face cream
People who judge him for not having a college degree
Men who he perceives as overly concerned with appearance

beliefs: Hard work is worth more than any degree
Men don't need skincare products — soap and water are fine
If something isn't broken, don't mess with it
You earn respect through what you do, not what you look like

general: Washes his face with whatever bar soap is in the shower
Has never purchased a skincare product in his life
Gets sunburned in summer but just deals with it
Would only consider a product if his skin was visibly, painfully problematic

morning: Up at 5:30, quick shower with Irish Spring
Breakfast is coffee and maybe a gas station sandwich on the way to the site
Throws on work clothes and steel-toe boots
workday: Physical labor on job sites — pulling wire, mounting panels
Lunch from a cooler or the nearest Sheetz
Learns from his uncle and the journeymen on site
Home by 4, sometimes later if the job runs over
evening: Showers to get the dust and sweat off
Dinner — usually something his mom made or frozen pizza
Video games with friends online or watches YouTube truck builds
In bed by 10 because 5:30 comes fast
weekend: Sleeps in until 8 (his version of sleeping in)
Works on his truck or ATV in the garage
Hunting or fishing depending on the season
Bonfire and beers at a friend's property

health.physical: Strong and fit from physical labor. Gets occasional windburn and chapped skin in winter that he ignores. Has a few acne scars from teenage breakouts he never treated. Doesn't wear sunscreen — ever.
health.mental: Emotionally steady and not prone to anxiety. Doesn't overthink things. Content with his path in life. Occasionally defensive when people question his career choice or lifestyle.

relationships.name: Steve Kowalski
relationships.description: Owner of the family electrical business. Tyler's main mentor and role model. Tough but fair. Also thinks skincare is nonsense.

relationships.name: Linda Kowalski
relationships.description: Works at a medical office. Has tried to get Tyler to at least use moisturizer in winter. He ignores her advice but loves her cooking.

relationships.name: Jake Brennan
relationships.description: Fellow trades apprentice (plumbing). They've been friends since middle school, hunt together, and play on the same Madden league.

relationships.name: Ashley Novak
relationships.description: Works at a hair salon in Scranton. She's the only person who's ever gotten him to use chapstick. Their relationship is casual and low-pressure.
